name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD organic/materials chemist with 6+ years in synthesis-informed materials characterization, DOE-guided process development, scale-up documentation, and analytical troubleshooting. Connects organic chemistry, LC-MS/NMR/GPC, spectroscopy, thermal analysis, and process controls to define reproducible chemistry and material-quality outcomes.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided bio-based polymer process development, identifying critical process parameters and translating material-quality data into reproducible process windows and scale-up specifications.

Designed, synthesized, and characterized organic molecular materials across 15+ variants, connecting structure, purity, stability, and performance through NMR/GPC, LC-MS, spectroscopy, thermal analysis, and electrochemistry.
Drove process development for AFOSR-funded molecular thin-film coatings, optimizing surface preparation and CVD conditions to control morphology, interfaces, and optical performance.
Optimized CVD polymer thin-film deposition with DOE and in-situ QCM-D monitoring, defining growth kinetics and process-control parameters for reproducible coatings.
Managed process-development and characterization lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented calibration, SOP, safety, and resource-planning systems that improved instrument uptime by 30%.

projects.title: DOE-Guided Process Development
projects.description: Built process windows and material-quality specifications for reproducible bio-based polymer scale-up.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, process parameters, technical reports

projects.title: Organic Materials Structure-Property Mapping
projects.description: Connected synthetic design, purity, stability, and performance across xanthene-based organic materials.
projects.tech: Organic synthesis, NMR, LC-MS, GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR

projects.title: AFOSR Thin-Film Scale-Up Controls
projects.description: Established process-property relationships for CVD/surface-preparation workflows and reproducible thin-film quality.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, surface prep, thin films

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.

skills.Analytical Characterization: LC-MS, GC-MS, NMR, GPC/SEC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, QCM-D, cyclic voltammetry
skills.Process & Documentation: DOE, SPC, JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel, protocols, technical reports, lab safety and quality systems

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award
